title: urena urens
intro: Urena urens, also known as the cocklebur or hogweed, is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae. Traditionally, Urena urens has been used in various folk medicines for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, contributing to its health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, Urena urens is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
